commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `text/vcard` to `HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES` in `src/media/web-media.ts` so host-read media sends can accept vCard contact files.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lacks `text/vcard` in the host-read allowlist, and `file-type` 22.0.1 reports vCards from a `BEGIN:VCARD` prefix. I did not run a local repro because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer revision because the PR changes a host-read security boundary and currently lacks both the safer text-validation fix and real-path proof.

target.title: fix(media): add text/vcard to host-read allowed document MIME types (#97062)
target.body:
Fixes #97062.

## Summary

- **Problem**: `text/vcard` (.vcf contact files, RFC 6350) is not in `HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES`, so sending a local .vcf file via `MEDIA:` directive or `message(media=...)` throws: `Host-local media sends only allow buffer-verified images, audio, video, PDF, and Office documents (got text/vcard)`.
- **Root cause**: `HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES` in `src/media/web-media.ts` does not include `text/vcard`.
- **Fix**: Added `"text/vcard"` to the allowlist set. VCF is a safe, text-based, RFC-standard format (similar to `text/csv` which is already allowed).
- **What did NOT change**: No matching or validation logic changed. Only the MIME allowlist set was extended.

## Change Type

- [x] Enhancement

## Scope

- [x] Media (host-read document MIME allowlist)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `text/vcard` MIME type is now accepted by the host-read document allowlist.

**Environment tested**: Node 24.13.1 on Linux, vitest.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx -e "
import { HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES } from './src/media/web-media.js';
console.log('text/vcard in allowlist:', HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES.has('text/vcard'));
"
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
text/vcard in allowlist: true
```

```bash
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/outbound-media.test.ts --run
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests      11 passed (11)
```

**Code change** (`src/media/web-media.ts`):
```diff
 const HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES = new Set([
   ...
   "text/csv",
   "text/markdown",
   "text/plain",
+  "text/vcard",
   "application/json",
   ...
 ]);
```

**Observed result after fix**: `.vcf` files can now be sent via `MEDIA:` or `message(media=...)` without hitting the host-read MIME block. The change is a single-entry addition to a Set — no behavioral impact on any other MIME type.

**What was not tested**: None. The Set membership is verified by direct function call, and existing test coverage validates the allowlist integration path.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/outbound-media.test.ts --run`
  - Test Files  1 passed (1)
  - Tests      11 passed (11)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/media/web-media.ts` — clean

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context)
- **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message
